pairs.question: Who was Charles Este and what were his ecclesiastical positions in Ireland?
pairs.answer: Charles Este (1696–1745) was a bishop in Ireland, serving first as the bishop of Ossory from 1735 to 1740, followed by the bishop of Waterford and Lismore from 1740 until his death in 1745.

pairs.question: What were the significant architectural contributions of Charles Este to the bishop's palaces in Kilkenny and Waterford?
pairs.answer: Charles Este restored the former bishop's palace in Kilkenny, which had fallen into disrepair by 1661, adding notable features such as a double staircase. He also commissioned the design of the former bishop's palace in Waterford, which featured Italianate windows, though the architect Richard Castle initially worked on the project before it was completed by John Roberts due to competing commitments.

pairs.question: How did Charles Este become associated with Archbishop Boulter of Armagh?
pairs.answer: Charles Este received the patronage of Archbishop Boulter of Armagh, who served as his mentor. Este began working under Boulter as his chaplain in 1724 and later moved to Ireland, where he was appointed to the rectory of Derrynoose in 1626.

pairs.question: What educational background did Charles Este have before his ecclesiastical career?
pairs.answer: Charles Este was educated at Westminster School and later at Christ Church, Oxford, where he distinguished himself as a joint editor of *Carmina quadragesimalia ab aedis Christi alumnis composita…* (1723), to which he also contributed.

pairs.question: What personal life events occurred for Charles Este around the time he became bishop of Ossory?
pairs.answer: Charles Este married Susanna Clements in May 1725. He was subsequently elevated to the archdeaconry of Armagh in 1730 and to the chancellorship of Armagh in 1733 before being nominated as bishop of Ossory in January 1736 and consecrated in February 1737.

pairs.question: Why was the bishop's palace in Waterford not completed during Charles Este's lifetime?
pairs.answer: The construction of the bishop's palace in Waterford, commissioned by Charles Este and designed by Richard Castle, faced delays when Castle abandoned the project to work for the Fitzgerald family on Leinster House. The commission was then passed to local architect John Roberts, and by the time the palace was completed, Este had already died on December 2, 1745.

pairs.question: Provide a timeline of Charles Este's key appointments and achievements in chronological order.
pairs.answer: Charles Este's key appointments and achievements include: born in 1696; educated at Westminster School and Christ Church, Oxford; married Susanna Clements in 1725; appointed chaplain to Archbishop Boulter in 1724; collated to the rectory of Derrynoose in 1626; raised to archdeacon of Armagh in 1730; became chancellor of Armagh in 1733; nominated as bishop of Ossory in January 1736 and consecrated in February 1737; moved to become bishop of Waterford and Lismore on July 18, 1744; died on December 2, 1745.
